concept_decisions.evidence_inspected: All 92 conflict titles and complete available abstracts; allowed concepts catalog; source concept table.
concept_decisions.rationale: The batch record routed here is a genomic diffusion reconstruction rather than Bayesian disease mapping, but Bayesian spatiotemporal disease models remain a distinct allowed concept and should be retained.

concept_decisions.labels_compared: contact_network_models vs phylogenetic_contact_network_inference, neural_network_prediction_models, neural_network_predictive_models, public_health_implementation_networks, exposure_pathway_network_models, sequence_similarity_network_models, gravity_radiation_mobility_models
concept_decisions.evidence_inspected: All 92 conflict titles and complete available abstracts; allowed concepts catalog; source concept table.
concept_decisions.rationale: This conflict set mixes true contact-network studies with phylodynamic network inference, implementation networks, exposure-pathway networks, gravity-style flow networks, and generic neural-network prediction papers, so the concept must be retained but split.

concept_decisions.evidence_inspected: All 92 conflict titles and complete available abstracts; allowed concepts catalog; source concept table.
concept_decisions.rationale: Only papers centered on explicit hotspot or space-time clustering methods fit this concept; several others are better treated as genomic epidemiology or phylogeographic diffusion studies.

concept_decisions.evidence_inspected: All 92 conflict titles and complete available abstracts; allowed concepts catalog; source concept table.
concept_decisions.rationale: The MaxEnt-style spatial risk mapping paper fits this concept, but the other records concern pathogen genomic evolution or pangenome structure rather than ecological-niche or species-distribution modeling.

concept_decisions.evidence_inspected: All 92 conflict titles and complete available abstracts; allowed concepts catalog; source concept table.
concept_decisions.rationale: No inspected batch record used endemic plus epidemic plus neighborhood decomposition, so this remains a distinct allowed concept rather than a synonym of other spatiotemporal model classes.

concept_decisions.evidence_inspected: All 92 conflict titles and complete available abstracts; allowed concepts catalog; source concept table.
concept_decisions.rationale: Across the batch, genomic epidemiology and transmission reconstruction is repeatedly distinct from purely phylogeographic movement studies, contact-network inference, and non-transmission population-genomic analyses, so it should be retained.

concept_decisions.evidence_inspected: All 92 conflict titles and complete available abstracts; allowed concepts catalog; source concept table.
concept_decisions.rationale: One record is a gravity-model extension, while another is a broader mobility-scaling law and flow-generation paper, so the concept should be retained but split from the more general mobility-flow-generation concept.

concept_decisions.labels_compared: metapopulation_models vs host_pathogen_metapopulation_ecology, phylogeography_genomic_diffusion, pathogen_population_genomics_within_host_evolution, genomic_epidemiology_transmission, mechanistic_statistical_hybrids, spatial_regression_geostatistics
concept_decisions.evidence_inspected: All 92 conflict titles and complete available abstracts; allowed concepts catalog; source concept table.
concept_decisions.rationale: The conflict set combines genuine mechanistic metapopulation transmission models with ecological metapopulation studies, pathogen population-genomic papers, phylogeographic reconstructions, and non-metapopulation predictive hybrids, so the concept must be split.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Pathogen population genomics and within-host evolution
reassignments.rationale: Multilocus sequence population-structure study, not a metapopulation transmission model.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Pathogen population genomics and within-host evolution
reassignments.rationale: Genome diversity and selection analysis rather than spatial clustering.
